title: ask Write(*) alone -> Write is pre-approval-gated (ask), not auto-allowed

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.

hypothesis: A bare ask rule on Write(*) puts the call into the ask state (group baseline). headless -> auto-deny (DENIED), SDK -> canUseTool fires (ASK), interactive -> approval prompt. SDK confirms engine=ask vs deny. As the group's representative, this case also verifies the RESULT axis (expected result: ok = 'approvable, completes when approved'): onAsk=allow makes the SDK callback approve the fired ask, so a single run observes BOTH askFired=[Write] (permission axis: ask) AND the side effect on disk (result axis: ok). Single-probe case, so the allow response cannot contaminate later probes; b/c/d keep the default onAsk=deny.
notes: MEASURED 2026-07-05: headless=DENIED, SDK=ASK (askFired=Write). engine=ask confirmed. spec-inventory §2 (3-value engine, order deny->ask->allow). RE-MEASURED with onAsk=allow: verifies the result axis (ok) — see results/sdk.json sideEffectsPresent. Closes P6 GAPS G5.
